sections.paragraph:
Χερούβ, τό (Ezk 28:16) and ὁ (Ex 25:19; 38:7: כְּרוּב) indecl. the image of the winged creature that stood over the covenant box, winged creature the pl. w. various endings Χερουβίν,-βείν (En 20:7; PGM 13, 255; 334),-βείμ,-βίμ (TestSol, TestAbrB; TestJob 50:2; ApcEsdr 2:26 p. 26, 20 Tdf. al.; ApcMos; SibOr 3, 1 τὰ Χερουβίμ; En 14:11 Χερουβὶν πύρινα; PGM 7, 634 ἐπὶ τὰ Χερουβίν; IDefixWünsch 3, 24 ἐπὶ τῶν Χερουβί; Ps 79:2 ἐπὶ τῶν Χερουβίν; En 20:7 Χερουβείν.—As sing. PGM 4, 3061 τοῦ Χερουβίν; 7, 264 ἐπὶ Χερουβίν), predom. neut. τά (Gen 3:24; Ex 25:18; 3 Km 6:27f; 8:7; 2 Ch 3:10, 13; Ezk 10:3, 8, 15; 11:22; 41:18, 20; En 14:11; ApcSed 2:26; ApcMos 19; Philo, Cher. 1; 25; 28, Fuga 100) more rarely masc. οἱ (Ex 25:19; 38:6f; Jos., Ant. 3, 137.—Elsewh. Joseph. writes Χερουβεῖς; indeed, he used the word as a masc., Ant. 7, 378 and as a fem. 8, 72f), of the two winged figures over the ark of the covenant Χερουβὶν δόξης Hb 9:5.—AJacoby, ARW 22, 1924, 257–65; PDhorme/LVincent, Les Chérubins: RB 35, 1926, 328–58; 481–95; TLL, Suppl. 1, 389–92.—BHHW I 298f. M-M. TW.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

  1. pl. = plural

En En = I Enoch—List 2

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

sing. sing. = singular

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

masc. masc. = masculine

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

fem. fem. = feminine

ARW ARW = Archiv für Religionswissenschaft—List 6

RB RB = Revue Biblique—List 6

TLL TLL = Thesaurus Linguae Latinae—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1084
